1.name: Easy
1.description: Factual questions directly from the text

2.name: Medium
2.description: Interpretive questions about meaning and themes

questions.question: Who had just died when the story of 2 Samuel begins?
questions.explanation: The book begins after the death of Saul.

questions.question: What news did the Amalekite bring to David?
questions.explanation: The Amalekite told David that Saul and Jonathan had died.

questions.question: What did the Amalekite bring to David from Saul?
questions.explanation: He brought Saul’s crown and bracelet.

questions.question: Whom did David lament in his funeral song?
questions.explanation: David lamented Saul and Jonathan.

questions.question: Where did the LORD tell David to go after the death of Saul?
questions.explanation: God told David to go to Hebron.

questions.question: Who was David anointed king over in Hebron?
questions.explanation: David was anointed king over the house of Judah at Hebron.

questions.question: Who became king over Israel in Mahanaim?
questions.explanation: Ish-bosheth, Saul’s son, was made king over Israel.

questions.question: Who were the three sons of Zeruiah?
questions.explanation: The sons of Zeruiah were Joab, Abishai, and Asahel.

questions.question: Where was Asahel buried?
questions.explanation: Asahel was buried in Bethlehem.

questions.question: Which child of David was born from Abigail?
questions.explanation: Chileab was David’s son by Abigail.

questions.question: Who was the son of Maacah?
questions.explanation: Absalom was the son of Maacah.

questions.question: Who was David’s fourth son?
questions.explanation: David’s fourth son was Adonijah.

questions.question: Who was the mother of David’s son Shephatiah?
questions.explanation: Shephatiah was born to Abital.

questions.question: Which son of David was born by Eglah?
questions.explanation: Ithream was the son of Eglah.

questions.question: Who was captain of Saul’s host?
questions.explanation: Abner was the captain of Saul’s army.

questions.question: Who did David request be returned to him from Ish-bosheth?
questions.explanation: David requested that Ish-bosheth return his wife Michal.

questions.question: How many men did Abner bring to David to make peace?
questions.explanation: Abner came to David with twenty men.

questions.question: Who killed Abner?
questions.explanation: Joab killed Abner when he met him privately.

questions.question: Where was Abner buried?
questions.explanation: David had Abner buried in Hebron.

questions.question: What happened to Ish-bosheth after Abner’s death?
questions.explanation: His hand became feeble and Israel was troubled.

questions.question: What was wrong with Jonathan’s son Mephibosheth?
questions.explanation: Mephibosheth was lame in both feet.

questions.question: How long did David reign over Judah at Hebron?
questions.explanation: David reigned in Hebron for seven years and six months.

questions.question: How old was David when he began to reign?
questions.explanation: David was thirty years old when he became king.

questions.question: What city did David capture and make his capital?
questions.explanation: David captured Jerusalem and made it his capital.

questions.question: What was gained by David from Jerusalem after its capture?
questions.explanation: He gained the stronghold of Zion in Jerusalem.

questions.question: Who was David’s wife mentioned after he came to Jerusalem?
questions.explanation: The text refers to Bathsheba, Uriah’s wife, as one of David’s wives.

questions.question: Which two nations attacked Israel after David became king?
questions.explanation: The Philistines attacked Israel after David became king.

questions.question: How did David bring the ark of the covenant to Jerusalem?
questions.explanation: He brought the ark with shouting, dancing, and celebration.

questions.question: Who touched the ark and was struck dead?
questions.explanation: Uzzah touched the ark to steady it and died.

questions.question: Which prophet spoke to David about his house?
questions.explanation: Nathan the prophet spoke to David about building a house for the ark.

questions.question: What promise did God make about David’s son?

options.A: He would be a son to God

questions.explanation: God said David’s son would be His son, and God would establish his throne forever.

questions.question: Which king did David defeat to secure his rule?
questions.explanation: David defeated Hadadezer, king of Zobah.

questions.question: What city did David take from the Syrians?
questions.explanation: He captured Tadmor from the Syrians.

questions.question: Who was David’s priestly advisor?
questions.explanation: Zadok was one of David’s priests and advisors.

questions.question: Whom did David seek out to show kindness for Jonathan’s sake?
questions.explanation: David sought any of Saul’s house to show kindness for Jonathan’s sake.

questions.question: Who did David find from Saul’s family?
questions.explanation: David found Mephibosheth, Jonathan’s son.

questions.question: Who was Mephibosheth's nurse?
questions.explanation: The text names Zibiah as Mephibosheth’s nurse.

questions.question: What city did David give to Mephibosheth?
questions.explanation: David restored Ziba’s fields and house to Mephibosheth.

questions.question: Who did David see bathing from his roof?
questions.explanation: David saw Bathsheba bathing on her roof.

questions.question: Who was Bathsheba married to when David saw her?
questions.explanation: Bathsheba was married to Uriah the Hittite.

questions.question: What excuse did Uriah give for not going home to his wife?

options.A: The ark and Israel and Judah are stayed in tents

questions.explanation: Uriah said he could not go home because the ark and people were encamped in tents.

questions.question: What did David do to Uriah to get him killed?

options.D: He put him in the front line of battle

questions.explanation: David had Uriah placed in the fiercest battle to die.

questions.question: Who became David’s wife after the death of Uriah?
questions.explanation: David married Bathsheba after Uriah died.

questions.question: Who confronted David over his sin with Bathsheba?
questions.explanation: Nathan the prophet confronted David.

questions.question: What punishment did God pronounce on David’s house?
questions.explanation: God said trouble would arise from David’s own house.

questions.question: What did David say after Nathan told him the LORD had put away his sin?

options.C: He had sinned against the LORD

questions.explanation: David confessed that he had sinned against the LORD.

questions.question: What happened to the child born to David and Bathsheba?
questions.explanation: The child became ill and died as part of the judgment.

questions.question: What was the name of the son born to David and Bathsheba after the death of their first child?
questions.explanation: They named their son Solomon.

questions.question: Who violated his half-sister Tamar?
questions.explanation: Amnon raped Tamar.

questions.question: Who killed Amnon?
questions.explanation: Absalom killed Amnon in revenge for Tamar.

questions.question: Why did the house of Judah anoint David but not all Israel initially?

options.A: Because Israel was divided and followed Ish-bosheth
options.C: Because David refused to reign over Israel
options.D: Because Judah did not trust David

questions.explanation: The kingdom was divided after Saul’s death, so Judah made David king first.

questions.question: What caused the fierce battle at Gibeon?

options.B: The rivalry between David and Ish-bosheth’s forces

questions.explanation: The conflict was between the forces loyal to David and those to Ish-bosheth.

questions.question: What drove Abner to switch allegiance toward David?

options.C: The desire to unify Israel under the LORD’s chosen king

questions.explanation: Abner sought to bring Israel together under David’s rule, claiming God had spoken of David.

questions.question: Why did Joab kill Abner despite David's peace efforts?

options.C: He acted out of personal rivalry alone
options.D: He sought revenge for his brother Asahel and distrusted Abner

questions.explanation: Joab killed Abner partly to avenge his brother Asahel and because he distrusted Abner.

questions.question: What kind of report did the men who killed Ish-bosheth bring to David?

options.B: Ish-bosheth was dead and David was avenged

questions.explanation: They claimed they had avenged David by killing Ish-bosheth.

questions.question: What does David’s age at ascension suggest about his preparation for kingship?

options.A: He had matured through years of shepherding and warfare
options.B: He was too young to rule
options.C: He became king immediately after Saul

questions.explanation: At thirty, David had years of life experience and leadership behind him.

questions.question: What strategic advantage did Jerusalem provide David?

options.A: It was close to the sea
options.B: It was already a religious center
options.C: Its stronghold and central location made it ideal for unifying the kingdom

questions.explanation: Jerusalem’s strong fortress and central position made it a strategic capital.

questions.question: Why was Uzzah’s death significant theologically?

options.A: It showed the ark was powerless
options.B: It emphasized God’s holiness and the need for proper reverence
options.C: It proved David was unfit to transport the ark

questions.explanation: God’s holiness required the ark to be treated according to His instructions.

questions.question: Why did David dance before the LORD with all his might?

options.A: He rejoiced that the ark was coming to Jerusalem
options.B: He sought to show off to the people
options.C: He wanted to insult the Philistines
options.D: He was performing a ritual to summon rain

questions.explanation: David celebrated the arrival of the ark with joyful dancing.

questions.question: What was the promise regarding David’s throne?

options.B: It would be established forever through his offspring
options.C: It would pass to a foreigner
options.D: It would last only during his lifetime

questions.explanation: God promised David that his throne would be established forever.

questions.question: What kind of justice did David administer?

options.C: Justice and righteousness for all his people

questions.explanation: David administered justice and righteousness across his realm.

questions.question: How did David show kindness to Mephibosheth?

options.A: He restored Saul’s land and invited him to eat at David’s table

questions.explanation: David showed loyalty to Jonathan by caring for his son.

questions.question: What was the moral failure at the heart of David’s sin with Bathsheba?

options.B: He coveted another man’s wife and abused his power

questions.explanation: David abused his royal authority and sinned against Uriah and Bathsheba.

questions.question: Why did David place Uriah in the front line?

options.C: To ensure Uriah would be killed and conceal his adultery

questions.explanation: David deliberately put Uriah in danger to hide his wrongdoing.

questions.question: How did Nathan make David see his sin?

options.D: By telling a parable about a rich man taking a poor man’s lamb

questions.explanation: Nathan used a parable to expose David’s heart and sin.

questions.question: What does David’s confession teach about repentance?

options.A: True repentance acknowledges sin against God

questions.explanation: David admitted his sin against the LORD, showing genuine repentance.

questions.question: What was the result of David’s failure to punish Amnon appropriately?

options.B: Absalom harbored hatred and later murdered Amnon

questions.explanation: David’s inaction led to further family tragedy and violence.

questions.question: Why did Absalom return to Jerusalem after several years?

options.A: Because Joab interceded for him with David
options.C: Because he was summoned by the people
options.D: Because he wanted to build a palace

questions.explanation: Joab arranged for Absalom’s return after intervening with David.

questions.question: What did Absalom do to gain public favor?

options.B: He acted as a judge at the gate and stole hearts by pledging support
options.C: He led the army into battle

questions.explanation: Absalom positioned himself as a friend to the people and was loved by them.

questions.question: Why did Absalom ask permission to go to Hebron?

options.C: To set up his rebellion against David

questions.explanation: He used the journey to Hebron to stage his rebellion.

questions.question: Who cursed David as he fled Jerusalem?
questions.explanation: Shimei cursed David during his flight from Absalom.

questions.question: Whose advice did David reject and which did he follow during Absalom’s rebellion?

options.A: He rejected Hushai and followed Ahithophel
options.B: He rejected Absalom and followed Shimei
options.C: He rejected Ahithophel and followed Hushai
options.D: He rejected Joab and followed Absalom

questions.explanation: David followed Hushai’s counsel to delay Absalolms advance and reject Ahithophel’s plan.

questions.question: What led to Absalom’s death?

options.A: His hair got caught in a tree while fleeing
options.B: He was killed in battle by David himself
options.C: He fell from his chariot in a stampede

questions.explanation: Absalom got caught by his hair in a tree and was killed by Joab.

questions.question: Whose report brought news of Absalom’s death to David?
questions.explanation: Hushai’s servant delivered the report to David.

questions.question: Why did David mourn for Absalom even after his rebellion?

options.A: Because he was his son and David loved him despite the rebellion
options.B: Because he feared the people’s reaction
options.C: Because he regretted losing the army
options.D: Because Absalom had been a great general

questions.explanation: David’s fatherly love caused him to mourn for Absalom.

questions.question: What was Joab’s criticism of David after Absalom’s death?

options.A: David was too harsh on Absalom
options.B: David should have fled the city earlier
options.C: David’s public mourning demoralized the troops and cost lives

questions.explanation: Joab said David’s mourning discouraged the men who had saved his life.

questions.question: What was Sheba’s rebellion about?

options.A: He said there was no share for him in David

questions.explanation: Sheba rebelled claiming he had no portion in David.

questions.question: Why did a famine come during David’s reign?

options.D: Because of Saul’s violation of God’s command against the Gibeonites

questions.explanation: The famine was connected to Saul’s violation of the Gibeonite covenant.

questions.question: What does the death of Abner and Ish-bosheth indicate about the early years of David’s rule?

options.A: That David personally killed all rivals
options.B: That his kingdom faced violent conflict and required consolidation

questions.explanation: David’s early reign included violent opposition and consolidation of power.

questions.question: What is the theme of David’s song of praise in chapter 22?

options.A: God is David’s rock, deliverer, and shield
options.D: A call to build a temple

questions.explanation: David’s song praises God as his protector and deliverer.

questions.question: Why is David’s consultation with the LORD before going to Hebron important?

options.A: It made him delay his reign
options.C: It showed his dependence on God’s guidance in establishing the kingdom
options.D: It was an attempt to avoid Joab

questions.explanation: David sought God’s direction for the next step in his kingship.

questions.question: How did capturing Jerusalem strengthen David’s political position?

options.B: It gave him a neutral and defensible capital respected by all tribes
options.C: It won him immediate peace with the Philistines
options.D: It made him ruler of Egypt

questions.explanation: Jerusalem was neutral territory and strategically strong for uniting Israel.

questions.question: Why did David not immediately bring the ark into his house after Uzzah died?

options.A: He was afraid because of the LORD’s anger
options.C: He wanted to consult with priests

questions.explanation: David feared the LORD after Uzzah’s death and temporarily left the ark at Obed-edom’s house.

questions.question: What is the long-term significance of God’s promise to David’s offspring?

options.A: It guaranteed immediate victory in battle
options.B: It removed all conflict from the kingdom
options.C: It promised wealth but not a dynasty
options.D: It established the Davidic dynasty and foreshadowed the Messianic line

questions.explanation: The promise pointed forward to a lasting dynasty and future Messiah.

questions.question: What does David’s victory over Hadadezer demonstrate about his leadership?

options.A: He was effective in expanding and securing Israel’s borders
options.C: He sought alliances with all neighbors
options.D: He was primarily a spiritual leader

questions.explanation: He strengthened Israel and defeated hostile powers during his reign.

questions.question: What does David’s kindness to Mephibosheth reveal about his character?

options.A: He only cared about political allies
options.B: He was loyal to his covenant with Jonathan and merciful to the house of Saul
options.C: He wanted to humiliate Saul’s household
options.D: He sought to weaken Saul’s line

questions.explanation: David showed covenant loyalty and mercy by caring for Jonathan’s son.

questions.question: How is David’s sin with Bathsheba linked to his failure as king?

options.A: He abused his power and neglected justice in his household
options.B: It only affected his personal life
options.C: It made him beloved by the people
options.D: It had no impact on the kingdom

questions.explanation: His personal sin reflected a lapse in righteous leadership.

questions.question: Why was Uriah’s death especially tragic morally?

options.D: Because David used him as a pawn to hide adultery

questions.explanation: Uriah was sacrificed by David to conceal his own sin.

questions.question: How does Nathan’s parable function as a prophetic indictment?

options.B: It explains the law in detail
options.C: It reveals David’s sin by analogy and makes him judge himself

questions.explanation: Nathan used a story to expose David’s wrongdoing indirectly and powerfully.

questions.question: Why did the child of David and Bathsheba die after seven days?

options.B: As divine judgment for David’s sin

questions.explanation: The child’s death was part of the judgment pronounced on David.

questions.question: What does the story of Amnon and Tamar show about the consequences of unchecked desire?

options.A: Uncontrolled lust can destroy family relationships and lead to violence

questions.explanation: Amnon’s sin set off a chain of family tragedy and rebellion.

questions.question: What does Absalom’s prolonged separation from his father reveal about David’s family dynamics?

options.A: That David prioritized political stability over family
options.B: That David struggled to exercise discipline and reconcile with his own children

questions.explanation: David’s weak response allowed deep family rifts to persist.

questions.question: How did Absalom’s public gestures undermine David’s authority?

options.A: By winning popular support and presenting himself as the people’s friend

questions.explanation: Absalom gained the people’s favor through a campaign of false intimacy.

questions.question: Why is Shimei’s cursing of David significant in the narrative?

options.A: It proves David is unpopular with all tribes
options.C: It reveals David’s humiliation and the severity of his exile

questions.explanation: Shimei’s abuse underscores the depth of David’s suffering and loss of honor.

questions.question: What does the LORD’s counsel to Hushai tell us about divine sovereignty in David’s crisis?

options.B: God can use even the counsel of the enemy to protect His servant

questions.explanation: God used Hushai’s advice and Absalom’s counselors to preserve David.

questions.question: Why did David react the way he did to the news of Absalom’s death?

options.A: Because he loved his son despite Absalom’s rebellion
options.B: Because he was angry at the messenger
options.C: Because he feared losing his throne
options.D: Because he wanted Absalom as king

questions.explanation: David mourned his son with deep grief even though Absalom had rebelled.

questions.question: What does David tell his followers after Absalom’s death?

options.A: He will punish all of them
options.B: He will act wisely toward all of Israel

questions.explanation: David sought reconciliation and wise judgment for all Israel.

questions.question: What does the fate of Sheba say about rebellion against David?

options.D: It led to a new dynasty

questions.explanation: Sheba’s rebellion was suppressed to maintain David’s rule.

questions.question: How does the covenant with the Gibeonites affect David’s reign?

options.A: It shows that past sins and broken promises can bring consequences for future generations
options.B: It proves the Gibeonites were enemies

questions.explanation: The famine was judged as a result of Saul’s broken covenant with the Gibeonites.

questions.question: How does David describe God in his song?

options.D: As my rock, fortress, and deliverer

questions.explanation: David calls God his rock, fortress, and deliverer.

questions.question: Why is David called the sweet psalmist of Israel?

options.A: Because of his poetic and prophetic songs

questions.explanation: David was renowned for composing psalms and songs of praise.

questions.question: What role did the three mighty men serve for David?
questions.explanation: The three were David’s bravest warriors and champions.

questions.question: What was the immediate cause of David’s decision to number Israel?

options.A: A desire for more tax revenue
options.B: The anger of the LORD and possibly Satan inciting him
options.C: A census requirement from the king of Egypt

questions.explanation: The text attributes the decision to the LORD’s anger and the incitement of Satan.

questions.question: Why did David choose to fall into the hand of the LORD rather than men?

options.A: He believed God’s mercy would be greater than human vengeance
options.C: He thought men would forgive him

questions.explanation: David trusted God’s mercy more than human retaliation.

questions.question: What did Araunah offer David?

options.A: A place to build a palace
options.D: His threshing floor and oxen for an offering

questions.explanation: Araunah offered David the threshing floor and oxen for sacrifice.

questions.question: What does David’s refusal of Araunah’s free offering show?

options.A: He did not want to sacrifice
options.C: He believed gifts to God should be bought and not given for free

questions.explanation: David insisted on buying the site as a genuine sacrifice to the LORD.

questions.question: What did David sacrifice before the LORD when the ark arrived in Jerusalem?
questions.explanation: David offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before the ark.

questions.question: What did David decide to do that displeased the LORD?

options.A: Take a census of Israel and Judah

questions.explanation: David counted Israel and Judah, which displeased the LORD.

questions.question: Why did David order the lamentation over Saul and Jonathan to be taught to the children of Judah?

options.B: To remember their glory and mourn their loss

questions.explanation: David wanted the lament to preserve the memory of Saul and Jonathan’s honorable sacrifice.

questions.question: What did David do before battling the Philistines at Baal-perazim?
questions.explanation: David sought God’s guidance before the battle.

questions.question: How long did the ark stay in the house of Obed-edom?
questions.explanation: The ark stayed with Obed-edom for three months.

questions.question: How did David react when God promised an eternal throne through his seed?

options.C: He praised God for His faithfulness

questions.explanation: David responded with praise and gratitude for God’s promise.

questions.question: Why did the capture of the chariots matter in David’s campaign?
questions.explanation: The capture reduced the effectiveness of Hadadezer’s forces and secured David’s victory.

questions.question: Why did David pay attention to the house of Saul?

options.A: Because of his covenant with Jonathan
options.B: Because Saul had promised him land
options.D: Because he wanted to build a palace there

questions.explanation: David honored his promise to Jonathan by caring for his son.

questions.question: What signified that David had taken Bathsheba as his wife?

options.A: She bore him a son named Solomon
options.B: She moved into the palace immediately

questions.explanation: Her marriage to David was indicated by the birth of Solomon.

questions.question: What would David’s house experience because of his sin?

options.B: Violence and calamity within his family

questions.explanation: God said calamity would arise from David’s own household.

questions.question: What was Tamar’s emotional state after being violated?
questions.explanation: Tamar was greatly distressed and felt violated by her brother Amnon.

questions.question: What condition accompanied Absalom’s return to Jerusalem?

options.A: He did not see David’s face for two years
options.B: He was immediately restored to the throne

questions.explanation: Absalom returned but was not allowed to see David’s face for two years.

questions.question: What was the political impact of Ziba’s report of Mephibosheth?

options.C: It led to war with the house of Saul
options.D: It caused David to distrust Mephibosheth temporarily

questions.explanation: David accepted Ziba’s report and later realized Mephibosheth had been loyal.

questions.question: How did David’s reaction to Absalom’s death contrast with the soldiers’ celebration?

options.A: David mourned even though his army was relieved
options.D: David punished the one who reported it

questions.explanation: David mourned his son while the army felt relief that the rebellion had ended.

questions.question: Why was David’s desire to build a house for the ark significant?

options.A: It showed he was abandoning worship
options.B: It reflected his desire to honor God but also revealed reliance on human plans for divine presence
options.D: It was a political move to impress neighbors

questions.explanation: David wanted to honor God, but God corrected him and shifted the focus to the dynasty instead.

questions.question: How does Zadok’s role as priest support David’s reign?

options.A: It provided spiritual legitimacy and continuity for David’s rule
options.B: It made David a high priest

questions.explanation: A faithful priest helped give David’s kingship religious legitimacy.

questions.question: How does David’s gift to Mephibosheth function as covenant memory?

options.C: As a living reminder of his covenant with Jonathan

questions.explanation: The gift preserved the memory of Jonathan and honored the covenant between friends.

questions.question: What does David’s marriage to Bathsheba after Uriah’s death illustrate about repentance and consequences?

options.B: It shows that sin has consequences even when forgiveness is granted

questions.explanation: David was forgiven but still faced consequences for his wrongdoing.

questions.question: Why was Nathan’s confrontation effective?

options.A: It exposed David’s hypocrisy by making him condemn the same behavior
options.B: It threatened David with the loss of his throne
options.C: It made David repent out of fear

questions.explanation: The parable made David judge his own sin before he knew it applied to him.

questions.question: Why did Absalom use a wise woman to petition David about reconciliation?

options.D: To indirectly appeal to David’s compassion

questions.explanation: The scheme used a respected mediator to influence David without direct confrontation.

questions.question: How does Shimei’s behavior represent public sentiment during Absalom’s rebellion?

options.A: It reveals that many in Israel saw David as a failed king
options.B: It suggests universal support for David
options.C: It shows Shimei was acting alone

questions.explanation: Shimei’s cursing reflected the unrest and disillusionment with David’s rule.

questions.question: What does the LORD’s involvement in Absalom’s counsel show about divine oversight?

options.C: God can use human plans to fulfill His purposes even amid rebellion
options.D: God only helps kings in Jerusalem

questions.explanation: God’s sovereignty extends even to the counsel of enemies when protecting His chosen king.

questions.question: How does Joab’s killing of Absalom differ from David’s earlier mercy?

options.A: Joab acted decisively and against the king’s command due to strategic necessity
options.D: Joab was acting under Absalom’s orders

questions.explanation: Joab killed Absalom to end the rebellion despite David’s instruction to deal gently with him.

questions.question: Why did David emphasize justice for all Israel after Absalom’s death?

options.B: To restore unity and show he was king of all Israel, not just Judah

questions.explanation: David sought to reestablish his rule over the entire nation and calm divisions.

questions.question: What role did Amasa play in the rebellion of Sheba?

options.C: He was appointed by David to replace Joab, but failed to act quickly

questions.explanation: Amasa was commissioned to lead David’s army but Joab ultimately carried out the pursuit.

questions.question: How does David’s solution to the Gibeonite grievance reflect ancient justice?

options.A: It shows retributive justice was still required to preserve covenant honor
options.B: It shows he valued mercy over justice
options.C: It indicates he ignored the covenant
options.D: It proves he sided with the Philistines

questions.explanation: David required execution of the guilty to uphold the broken covenant with the Gibeonites.

questions.question: What does David’s claim that God gives him great salvation imply about his theology?

options.A: He relied mainly on his own strength
options.B: He saw salvation as military success alone
options.C: He believed his victories were manifestations of God’s saving power

questions.explanation: David attributed his deliverances to God’s saving action.

questions.question: How do the mighty men in David’s final words contribute to his legacy?

options.A: They show he had little support
options.B: They highlight the personal loyalty and bravery that sustained his kingship

questions.explanation: David’s warriors exemplify the loyal support that enabled his reign.

questions.question: Why did David choose the LORD’s punishment over human punishment?

options.A: He accepted responsibility and trusted God’s mercy even in judgment
options.B: He feared men more than God
options.D: He wanted to appease the people

questions.explanation: David sought God’s mercy by accepting divine punishment.

questions.question: What principle about worship and sacrifice does David demonstrate at Araunah’s threshing floor?

options.B: God deserves the best and sacrifice should be costly, not merely convenient
options.C: It is enough to bring something of little value

questions.explanation: David paid for the sacrifice site and animals, showing true worship cost something.

questions.question: Which giant fell by the hand of Sibbechai?
questions.explanation: Sibbechai killed Ishbi-benob, a Philistine giant.

questions.question: Which of David’s mighty men killed a Philistine giant at Bethlehem?
questions.explanation: Benaiah killed a Philistine at Bethlehem and a lion in a pit.

questions.question: How did David feel after he numbered the people?
questions.explanation: David was greatly distressed after he took the census.

questions.question: Who appeared to David on the threshing floor of Ornan?
questions.explanation: Gad told David to build an altar on Ornan’s threshing floor.

questions.question: What did David take from the Syrians besides chariots and horses?
questions.explanation: David seized large quantities of brass from the Syrians.

questions.question: What meaning did Bathsheba give to the name Solomon?
questions.explanation: Bathsheba said his name would be called Solomon because the LORD had given her rest.

questions.question: Why did Hushai go back to Jerusalem instead of following David?

options.A: To act as a spy and counter Absalom’s counselors
options.D: To deliver a message to Joab

questions.explanation: Hushai returned to thwart Absalom’s plans from within his court.

questions.question: What condition did David set for his commanders in battle against Absalom?

options.C: Deal gently for my sake, for he is my son

questions.explanation: David instructed them to be gentle with Absalom because he was his son.

questions.question: What was David’s leadership challenge after Absalom’s defeat?

options.B: To govern and reconcile a divided nation

questions.explanation: David needed to rebuild unity and legitimacy across Israel.

questions.question: What did Sheba’s rebellion highlight about Israel’s political stability?

options.B: There was no longer any dissent
options.C: The Philistines were controlling the land
options.D: David’s rule still faced internal opposition

questions.explanation: Sheba’s revolt showed David still had rivals and unrest.

questions.question: What role did Sibbechai play in Israel’s battles?

options.A: He killed a giant and helped secure victory

questions.explanation: Sibbechai killed a Philistine giant and was counted among the mighty men.

questions.question: What does David say about God giving him salvation among the nations?

options.A: God delivers him from adversaries and exalts his people
options.B: God only cares for Israel’s enemies

questions.explanation: David credited God with giving him great salvation and strength over nations.

questions.question: How does David’s final oracle connect with his identity as king?

options.A: It affirms him as the anointed of the LORD and a prophet of God

questions.explanation: David’s oracle highlights his unique role as God’s chosen ruler and psalmist.

questions.question: What is the significance of David building an altar on Araunah’s threshing floor?

options.A: It becomes the birthplace of David’s dynasty
options.B: It foreshadows the future temple site and proper worship under God’s terms

questions.explanation: David’s altar on the threshing floor foreshadows the temple site and genuine worship.
